items.title: VI Live: NEC en Volendam begonnen voor plek in de halve finale
items.summary: Op woensdagavond staan er opnieuw twee wedstrijden in de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker op het programma. PSV speelt in eigen huis tegen SC Heerenveen. NEC krijgt bezoek van FC Volendam. Volg de bekeravond in deze VI Live.

items.title: Winteroverzicht: hoeveel transfers waren er en hoe deden de topclubs het?
items.summary: Nu Deadline Day achter de rug is kan de transferbalans opgemaakt worden. In totaal noteerde de KNVB 228 transfers deze window, maar hoe staan de topclubs ervoor?

items.title: Jango Baie to suit truly-run Gold Cup, says De Boinville on Unbridled
items.summary: Nico de Boinville joined the latest episode of Unbridled, looking ahead to the coming weeks and months - starting with Super Saturday from Newbury this weekend.

items.title: Lang legt uit waarom hij bij Napoli vertrok: 'Je moet eerlijk worden behandeld'
items.summary: Noa Lang heeft een boekje opengedaan over zijn redenen om na een halfjaar alweer te vertrekken bij Napoli. De buitenspeler verruilde Zuid-Italië in januari voor Istanboel, waar hij zijn duels nu speelt voor Galatasaray. 'Ik weet dat jullie in Nederland vaak denken dat het aan mij ligt, maar op een dag wordt het nog wel bekend.'

items.title: Bad Bunny and the Bay: Why San Francisco is leading the way in sport
items.summary: "We are more LGF, than LFG."

items.title: Sources: Unhappy Ronaldo to boycott 2nd game
items.summary: Al Nassr star Cristiano Ronaldo will maintain his stance and boycott a second-straight league game after not receiving a concrete guarantee that the Saudi Arabia PIF will make changes regarding the Saudi Pro League club's management, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Arsenal's Zubimendi rejects claim Madrid need him
items.summary: Arsenal midfielder Martín Zubimendi has denied speculation in Spain that he is the player that Real Madrid needs.

items.title: Newell's have dream plan to lure Messi in 2027
items.summary: Newell's Old Boys are working on a project to entice Lionel Messi to play for the club in the first half of 2027.

items.title: Arteta: 'Magical' final is 'best vitamins' for Arsenal
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has described Arsenal 's "magical" achievement in reaching the Carabao Cup Final as "the best vitamins we can put in our bodies."

items.title: Celtic’s match at Aberdeen postponed and Dundee v Motherwell also rained off
items.summary: Pitches at Pittodrie and Dens Park waterlogged Celtic lose chance to close gap after Hearts’ loss Celtic’s Scottish Premiership match at Aberdeen has been postponed, along with Dundee v Motherwell, after heavy rain led to the pitches becoming waterlogged. Aberdeen confirmed shortly before 4.30pm (GMT) that the Pittodrie pitch was not suitable for play. Celtic were hoping to close in on leaders Hearts and could have moved to within three points. Dundee have struggled with the Dens Park pitch over the past two years and it again fell victim to the heavy rain. Continue reading...

items.title: Doelmannendiscussie in Marseille: verdringt De Lange Rulli onder de lat?
items.summary: Bij Olympique Marseille kon wel eens een keeperswissel in de maak zijn. Oud-Ajacied Gerónimo Rulli is eerste keus bij de ploeg van trainer Roberto De Zerbi, maar de Nederlandse Jeffrey de Lange kon de Argentijn wel eens onder de lat verdrijven. In aanloop naar de topper tegen Paris Saint-Germain is het dé vraag die boven de opstelling van l'OM hangt.

items.title: Soccer's newest superagent is doing things differently -- and he wants you to know it
items.summary: Ali Barat is impossible to miss -- last summer's transfer window was dominated by his clients making big moves -- yet hard to track down. It's all part of his mystique.

items.title: Dit is het speelschema voor de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker
items.summary: De KNVB heeft het speelschema voor de kwartfinale van de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker bekendgemaakt. Landskampioen PSV speelt op woensdag, titelverdediger Go Ahead Eagles sluit de bekerweek af.

items.title: Aberdeen v Celtic & Dundee v Motherwell games off
items.summary: Celtic's Scottish Premiership visit to Aberdeen is postponed, along with Dundee's match with Motherwell, after heavy rain in the north east.

items.title: Inside Man City's rebuild and plan for life without Guardiola
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at Manchester City's evolution and why the January window has offered renewed belief.

items.title: Andy Carroll denies breaching non-molestation order related to ex-wife
items.summary: Carroll allegedly repeatedly called Billi Mucklow last March Former England striker to next appear in court in March The former England footballer Andy Carroll has denied breaching a non-molestation order by allegedly repeatedly calling his former wife. Carroll, a former striker at Premier League sides Newcastle and Liverpool, indicated a not guilty plea to the charge at Chelmsford magistrates court on Wednesday. Carroll is charged with breaching the order made by the family court at Chelmsford justice centre by allegedly repeatedly calling in March last year Billi Mucklow, who has featured on reality TV show The Only Way Is Essex. Continue reading...

items.title: Aberdeen vs Celtic postponed due to waterlogged pitch
items.summary: Celtic's hopes of cutting the gap to Hearts at the top of the Scottish Premiership to three points have hit a snag after their trip to Aberdeen, due to be shown live on Sky Sports, was postponed less than four hours before kick-off.

items.title: Ronaldo expected to end his Saudi strike
items.summary: Senior Saudi football officials are now expecting Cristiano Ronaldo to end his strike and play against Al Ittihad on Friday.

items.title: Football Daily | Arsenal’s fun boat sails on but quadruple attempt will surely hit the rocks
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! Given their near misses in recent seasons, it is entirely understandable that most sentient Arsenal fans are not prepared to publicly entertain the notion that their team will almost certainly win the Premier League this season. While they’re all lying in bed at night secretly fantasising about Martin Ødegaard’s trophy lift, most remain too scarred by ridicule over perceived “bottle jobs” to confidently state that as far as the title is concerned, nothing can possibly go wrong. While they have gladly accepted Mikel Arteta’s invitation to jump on the fun boat, all are wearing life preservers. Quite what these same Arsenal fans make of various pundits blithely weighing up their chances of winning an unprecedented quadruple is anyone’s guess, but since they booked their place in the Fizzy Cup final, the external chatter has begun. Having won the square root of eff all in over five years, assorted experts are seriously suggesting Arsenal – Arsenal! – could win four shiny pots in the next four months. I have to feel sorry for the Ipswich fans who made plans to visit Fratton Park last night with the game being called off for the second time (frozen pitch previously, now waterlogged). Not sure how we are going to stop the next rearrangement if it doesn’t suit our knack-list but I hear floodlights do lose power sometimes” – Ben North (and no other devious Pompey fans). No disrespect to anyone – least of all Matt Atkinson – but comparing Timo Werner to the Yorkshire Stakhanovite that is James Milner (Monday’s Football Daily letters) is like comparing a pony to a thoroughbred” – Kev McCready. This is an extract from our daily football email … Football Daily. To get the full version, just visit this page and follow the instructions. Continue reading...

items.title: Harper is the fight Dubois has been waiting for: 'So much on the line'
items.summary: Caroline Dubois has finally got the fight she has been waiting for after her lightweight world title unification bout with Terri Harper was confirmed.
